{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "degraded"],
  "percent_complete": 95,
  "value": 23.4,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Left front brake rotor thickness measured 23.4 mm, which is below the stamped minimum thickness of 24.0 mm",
    "Left front caliper brake hose installed with a half twist between hard line and caliper"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace left front brake rotor immediately; do not return vehicle to service",
    "Relieve twist in left front brake hose to prevent restriction, wear, and potential rupture"
  ],
  "rationale": "Rotor below minimum thickness cannot dissipate heat safely, risking fade, warping, or fracture. Twisted hose can restrict fluid flow, trap pressure, or chafe and fail. Both defects make the vehicle unsafe despite the firm pedal during a low-speed test.",
  "references": [
    "OEM stamped minimum rotor thickness 24.0 mm",
    "Typical brake service best practice: brake hoses must be installed without twists or kinks"
  ]
}
